methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Candela is a Series C-stage transportation company that has raised €129M+ to date. The relevant peer universe comprises seven private companies in the transportation sector, including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft. No public transportation comparables exist at sufficient scale or stage similarity to inform valuation.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this analysis. A review of same-stage peers within transportation yielded only two datapoints, below the three-company minimum threshold required for reliable median-based valuation. No adjacent-stage or similar-tier private peers were available with sufficient comparability. Attempting to broaden the screen to all-sector transportation peers would introduce substantial heterogeneity in business model, capital intensity, and market maturity that would undermine analytical validity. Given this constraint, traditional multiples-based triangulation cannot be reliably executed.

In the absence of sufficient peer density or public market anchors, valuation of Candela requires alternative methodologies not captured in the provided data—such as discounted cash flow analysis, venture capital method application, or transaction-based benchmarking of recent fundraising rounds. The fundamental constraint is the thinly populated frontier-tech subsector, which limits the ability to triangulate value through conventional comparable-company approaches. Any valuation estimate at this stage should explicitly flag the reliance on private funding history and the absence of public market signals or dense peer comparability.
